content.claim: Geographic and rural-urban disparities in access to extended-track gimnáziums layer on socioeconomic selection; distance to nearest 6- or 8-year track school is used as a causal instrument because proximity is not randomly distributed and clusters in urban areas.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Geographic access disparities are inferred from methodological choices in causal research rather than directly quantified access statistics
Does not provide specific information on rural vs. urban participation rates in selective tracks
Distance as an instrument assumes no other correlation between school location and family characteristics
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
